info.name: Uncord Server
info.description:
API collection for the Uncord self-hosted chat server.

After registering or logging in, the access token is automatically saved to the collection variable `token` and used for all authenticated requests.

Protected endpoints enforce two additional gates beyond JWT authentication:

1. Email verification: users who have not verified their email receive 403 with error code `EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED`.
2. Active membership: users who have not completed server onboarding receive 403 with error code `MEMBERSHIP_REQUIRED`. A limited set of endpoints (own profile, join via invite, accept onboarding, check own member status, leave server, list channels) remain accessible to pending members.

query.description: Number of entries to return (1-100, default 50)
Cursor for pagination (audit log entry ID)
Filter by the user who performed the action
Filter by action type (e.g. role.create, member.ban)
Cursor for pagination (user ID of last item)
Number of members to return (1-100, default 50)

request.description:
POST /api/v1/channels/:channelID/typing

Notifies other clients that the authenticated user has started typing in the channel. Returns 204 No Content. Duplicate calls within 10 seconds are silently deduplicated.

DELETE /api/v1/channels/:channelID/typing

Clears the typing indicator for the authenticated user in the channel. Returns 204 No Content. Idempotent: returns 204 even if the user was not typing.

PUT /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Adds a unicode reaction to a message. The emoji parameter is a URL-encoded unicode character (e.g. %F0%9F%91%8D for thumbs up).

PUT /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Adds a custom emoji reaction to a message. The emoji parameter uses the format custom:{emojiID}.

DELETE /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Removes the authenticated user's reaction from a message. The emoji parameter is a URL-encoded unicode character.

DELETE /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Removes the authenticated user's custom emoji reaction from a message.

GET /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions

Returns grouped reaction summaries for a message, including emoji identifier, count, and whether the authenticated user has reacted.

GET /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Returns the list of users who reacted with the specified unicode emoji.

GET /api/v1/channels/:channelID/messages/:messageID/reactions/:emoji

Returns the list of users who reacted with the specified custom emoji.
Returns the onboarding configuration and all documents. No active membership required so clients can discover requirements before completing them.
Returns the next step the authenticated user must complete in the onboarding flow. Possible steps: verify_email, join_server, accept_documents, complete.
Updates the onboarding configuration. Only the server owner may call this endpoint. Documents are managed on the filesystem, not via this endpoint.
Validates that all required documents have been accepted, then activates the pending member.
Allows users to join the server without an invite when open_join is enabled in the onboarding configuration.

item.name: Threads
item.description: Thread creation, listing, updating, and thread message management.

request.description: Creates a thread on a message. Requires the CreateThreads permission on the parent channel.
Lists all threads in a channel. Requires ViewChannels permission.
Returns a single thread by ID. Requires ViewChannels permission on the parent channel.
Updates a thread's name, archived, or locked state. Requires ManageChannels permission on the parent channel. Locked threads reject changes unless the update explicitly unlocks them.
Lists messages in a thread with cursor-based pagination. Requires ViewChannels and ReadMessageHistory permissions on the parent channel.
Sends a message in a thread. Requires SendMessagesThreads permission. Archived and locked threads reject new messages.

item.name: Pins
item.description: Pin and unpin messages, and list pinned messages in a channel.

request.description: Pins a message. Requires ManageMessages permission on the channel.
Unpins a message. Requires ManageMessages permission on the channel.
Lists all pinned messages in a channel. Requires ViewChannels permission.

item.name: Gateway
item.description: WebSocket gateway for real-time events. Connect via `ws://localhost:9800/api/v1/gateway`. After the upgrade, the server sends a Hello frame (op 10) with the heartbeat interval. The client must respond with Identify (op 2, with a JWT token) or Resume (op 6) within 30 seconds.

request.description: Upgrades to a WebSocket connection. Authentication happens inside the WebSocket via Identify (op 2) after the upgrade completes.
